title: Host-neutral deterministic routing rules for Loop Marketing v2

normative_language.MUST: mandatory; violation rejects or blocks the route
normative_language.SHOULD: default that may be overridden only by a recorded decision with evidence
normative_language.MAY: optional behavior that does not change ownership or invariants

in_scope: select one current bottleneck or a deterministic diagnostic fallback
gate work by maturity and available evidence
plan specialist sequence and safe parallelism
select zero to two preserved canonical tactics per invoked pillar
resolve ownership, state, delivery and recommendation collisions
classify Refinar results over complete benchmark intervals
out_of_scope: editing or semantically merging any canonical prompt
implementing runtime, persistence, security adapters or provider integrations
defining the final 100-item tactic catalog that belongs to P3
performing external mutations or inferring authorization from a route
scope.host_neutrality: Rules use canonical role IDs, project IDs, state revisions and evidence references; no rule depends on Claude, Codex, a host-specific memory path or a host-specific command name.

supports: maturity dimensions and four ordered levels
facts, user interpretations and symptoms are distinct
four-pillar weighted scoring and secondary threshold
root-cause check may override an unreconciled symptom score
sequence, parallelism, Refinar checkpoint and planner handoff inputs
cross-validation, state evidence, claim provenance and scope reversion
normalized diagnosis, route output and outside-loop prerequisites
failure modes for diagnosis, sequence, maturity, evidence and external prerequisites
index-first progressive disclosure and one-to-two tactic wording
maturity gating and no forced tactic match
index-first tactic selection, no repeated tests and explicit simultaneous-test coordinator
maturity prerequisites and blocked advanced methods
eligibility, exclusions and historical timing ownership conflict
touchpoint overlap, orphan and ownership detection
delivery spacing, fallback, global frequency cap and delivery priority
handoff inputs, respected decisions and mandatory scope boundary
handoff inputs, respected decisions and mandatory scope boundary
handoff inputs, respected decisions and mandatory scope boundary
handoff inputs, respected decisions and mandatory scope boundary
benchmark source priority and no-benchmark behavior
baseline Refinar thresholds and the 50-to-60 percent gap
test design, canonical baseline states and evidence-only advancement
project test fields, state list and learned-result record
100 prompts remain a separate execution layer selected through indexes

field_rules.project_id: canonical identifier independent of display name and host path
field_rules.state_revision: non-negative integer used for optimistic concurrency
field_rules.user_goal: one explicit outcome sentence; multiple goals MUST be split or ranked
field_rules.observations: array of claims conforming to claim_contract
field_rules.available_capabilities: declared data, tools and implementation capabilities; absence means unavailable, not false
field_rules.authorization_context: read and write permissions by operation; routing never upgrades authorization

provenance.forbidden_behavior: A hypothesis MUST NOT be copied into facts merely because it was repeated by a user or agent.

pillar_score_shape.signals: four weighted true, false or unknown observations with evidence refs

root_cause_shape.causal_chain: three explicit why steps or a recorded data gap

primary_bottleneck_shape.pillar: exactly one pillar or null when route_status is needs_evidence or blocked
primary_bottleneck_shape.selection_rule_id: rule that selected or withheld it

normalization.rule: Every observation MUST be normalized as fact, user_interpretation, symptom or hypothesis before scoring.
Only facts may directly set a scoring signal to true or false; interpretations and hypotheses may create a data request but cannot add score.
A route MUST preserve the input state revision; any accepted state write uses compare-and-swap against that revision.

classification_precedence.when: an evidenced maturity classification is at most 30 days old and no structural-change event is recorded
the maturity reuse condition in RTE-MAT-001 is false
classification_precedence.result: recompute from the maturity decision table

decision_table.when: none of the prior rows match

tactic_gate_rules.rule: A tactic is eligible only when project maturity rank is greater than or equal to the tactic minimum_maturity rank and every tactic prerequisite is evidenced.

Existing [EM DESENVOLVIMENTO+], [MADURO+] and [AVANÇADO] index markers map to minimum ranks 1, 2 and 3. Absence of a marker is not proof of universal applicability; missing P3 metadata prevents that tactic from being selected but does not prevent use of the specialist base method.

selection_precedence.when: a necessary RevOps, data engineering, migration, instrumentation or workflow prerequisite is absent
root_cause_candidate has medium or high confidence, at least one supporting fact and no unresolved stronger counter-evidence
selection_precedence.result: loop_planning accepts its owner pillar as primary_bottleneck after reconciling any score mismatch

selection_precedence.when: one pillar confirmed_score is greater than every other pillar possible_score
confirmed top score is unique and all top-score signals are known
selection_precedence.result: select the unique top pillar; pillars with confirmed_score >= 5 are secondary

selection_precedence.when: tie includes Refinar and the failure locus is unknown and measurable data exists
selection_precedence.result: select Refinar as diagnostic checkpoint, then reroute

selection_precedence.when: failure locus is already factually localized to a structural pillar and measurement readiness is false
selection_precedence.result: select the structural owner; schedule Refinar after minimum instrumentation exists

selection_precedence.when: no prior rule selects one pillar and evidence is insufficient or tied
selection_precedence.result: route_status=needs_evidence; primary_bottleneck=null; next_action=Refinar in minimo_viavel data-audit mode

authority_rule.rule: Only loop_planning may accept or replace primary_bottleneck. Specialists may emit bottleneck_change_proposal with evidence but MUST NOT write the accepted bottleneck.

decision_table.condition: ampliar is requested and message_validated == false
ampliar is requested and audience_eligibility_defined == false
verbalizar and orientar are both selected, share only read inputs and neither requires the other's new output
decision_table.integration: loop_planning validates and merges staged outputs

decision_table.condition: verbalizar needs newly defined segments or orientar needs newly defined message hierarchy
decision_table.required_order: the producer of the explicitly required input runs first

decision_table.condition: measurable historical or live output exists and failure locus is unknown
new work from verbalizar, orientar or ampliar must be evaluated

parallelism_invariants: Parallel nodes MUST read the same immutable state_revision.
Parallel nodes MUST have non-overlapping decision owners and staged write sets.
No specialist may directly integrate another specialist's output.
The orchestrator MUST cross-validate all staged outputs before one compare-and-swap integration.
Simultaneous experiments MUST use disjoint audiences, disjoint isolated variables or an explicit multivariate interaction design owned by Refinar.
A parallel node whose handoff becomes stale MUST be revalidated; it cannot silently replay on a newer revision.

tactic_selection.library_invariant: The router references canonical tactic IDs and paths only. It MUST preserve all 100 prompts and MUST NOT delete, merge, rewrite, rename or treat two overlapping prompts as one.

cardinality_rules.when: the specialist base method is sufficient, no exact catalog match exists, metadata is incomplete or every match is gated
cardinality_rules.behavior: run the base method; do not force a tactic

cardinality_rules.when: one eligible tactic covers the requested tactical output

when_all: two distinct required outputs remain after the first selection OR the first tactic declares the second as a dependency
both tactics pass maturity, prerequisite and contraindication gates
their declared outputs and write sets do not collide
the second is not merely a semantic duplicate of the first

cardinality_rules.when: more than two tactics are requested for one pillar in one cycle

ranking: eligible exact output_contract match before partial match
more required need_tags matched before fewer
fewer missing optional inputs before more
canonical_tactic_id ascending as a stable non-semantic final tie-break

decision_table.collision: two roles decide the same owned field
decision_table.resolution: retain the canonical owner's value; convert the non-owner value to a proposal; block if it cannot be represented as a proposal

decision_table.collision: two parallel outputs write the same state field
decision_table.resolution: do not merge; return both to loop_planning with ERR_PARALLEL_WRITE_COLLISION

resolution_order: Orientar exclusion or suppression blocks the contact
Refinar experiment assignment isolates an eligible sample but cannot bypass suppression
Ampliar applies spacing, fallback and the global delivery cap
Ampliar delivery priority chooses among still-eligible touchpoints while preserving Orientar business-priority metadata

decision_table.collision: new recommendation contradicts an accepted decision
decision_table.resolution: accepted decision remains active unless the canonical owner submits explicit superseding evidence and loop_planning records the supersession

decision_table.collision: fact-backed output conflicts with hypothesis-only output
decision_table.resolution: fact-backed output prevails; preserve the hypothesis as unresolved counter-hypothesis

decision_table.collision: same-owner recommendations conflict with equally ranked evidence
decision_table.resolution: route_status=needs_evidence; no arbitrary winner; request discriminating evidence or a Refinar test

decision_table.collision: two simultaneous tests share audience and may interact
decision_table.resolution: serialize them or require Refinar to define a multivariate interaction design and allocation plan

always_required_checks.block_when: a decision or experiment state advanced without the transition evidence required by this contract
a factual claim lacks a source reference or an inference lacks an explicit hypothesis marker
an output decides a field owned by another role
a recommendation exceeds evidenced maturity or available implementation capability
the route changes prompt count, canonical path or canonical content hash

multi_output_checks.result: warning when outputs are compatible but create a material tension; block when they cannot both be executed
block silent contradiction; allow only an explicit supersession event
block a consumer whose producer handoff did not pass validation

result_contract.close_cycle_condition: All always-required checks pass and no multi-output check is block or reject. Warnings must be recorded in the integrated route.

validation_rules.rule: All required fields MUST be non-empty except known_data_gaps, which MUST be an array and may be empty.
The state revision MUST equal the revision the target will read; otherwise revalidate or regenerate the handoff.
The scope boundary MUST include every owner field not controlled by the target role.
The objective and expected output MUST fit the target owner matrix and MUST NOT instruct the target to overwrite the bottleneck.
Every factual input MUST have provenance; every unsupported interpretation MUST remain a hypothesis.
A target may start only after all declared dependencies have a validated handoff or are explicitly marked not_applicable.

benchmark_resolution_order: documented_market_benchmark with source and comparable definition
best comparable own campaign or flow

requirements: observed and benchmark MUST use the same definition, cohort and window
non-comparable or missing benchmark produces TESTAR rather than an invented index

when_any: no comparable benchmark can be resolved
sample is below a predeclared minimum
the causal variable was not isolated and no explicit multivariate design exists
metric definition, direction or denominator is invalid

all_additional_conditions: positive trend for at least 2 consecutive comparable periods
cost per result is stable or decreasing
below threshold for at least 2 consecutive comparable periods
at least 1 isolated optimization attempt completed without material improvement

classification_precedence.when: PARAR prerequisites are not all satisfied

classification_precedence.note: This row closes the P1-R013 gap without weakening the stricter PARAR evidence requirements.

classification_precedence.when: one or more ESCALAR additional conditions are not satisfied

required_evidence: hypothesis with specific change and measurable expected result
one isolated variable or explicit multivariate design
numeric success metric and target set before launch
observation window or predeclared early-stop trigger

transition_invariants: No forward state may be skipped.
Restarting or rerouting the loop never changes experiment state.
Approval text without actor, scope and timestamp is not an approval event.
Instrumentation plans are not instrumentation evidence.
A planned launch is not a launch event.
A completed state requires evidence that the experiment actually ran; cancellation is not completion.
Backward mutation is forbidden; corrections append an invalidation or create a new experiment with derived_from.
Every transition is an append-only event with event_id, experiment_id, from, to, actor, occurred_at and evidence_refs.
Only Refinar owns experiment design and success metrics; external execution still requires its own explicit authorization.

invariants.rule: All 100 canonical prompts remain present, individually identifiable and unchanged; routing changes only references and external metadata.
Exactly one accepted primary bottleneck exists when route_status is ready; zero exists when status is needs_evidence, blocked or rejected.
Only loop_planning accepts bottleneck, sequence, integrated route and supersession decisions.
A role never decides a field outside the owner matrix; it may only submit a sourced proposal to the owner.
No factual output lacks provenance and no hypothesis is serialized as fact.
No state transition occurs without a valid event and required evidence.
Routing, planning and handoff never imply authorization for an external write.
A route cannot close until cross-validation passes for state, provenance, owner scope, maturity and library integrity.
Decision rules are normative and versioned; hypotheses are non-normative and cannot alter routing until promoted by an explicit decision.

rejection_codes.meaning: required route input is missing
rejection_codes.remedy: supply the named field

rejection_codes.meaning: fact lacks source or hypothesis lacks rationale
rejection_codes.remedy: attach provenance or relabel as hypothesis

rejection_codes.meaning: score or bottleneck relies on interpretation or hypothesis as fact
rejection_codes.remedy: obtain evidence or remove the score contribution

rejection_codes.meaning: route or write is based on an obsolete project revision
rejection_codes.remedy: rehydrate and rerun validation

rejection_codes.meaning: required RevOps or data foundation is absent
rejection_codes.remedy: record and satisfy the prerequisite outside the pillar route

rejection_codes.meaning: available evidence cannot select one bottleneck
rejection_codes.remedy: run Refinar minimum-viable data audit or collect discriminating evidence

rejection_codes.meaning: a specialist attempted to accept a bottleneck change
rejection_codes.remedy: convert the change to a proposal for loop_planning

rejection_codes.meaning: ready route has zero or multiple accepted bottlenecks
rejection_codes.remedy: apply bottleneck precedence or mark needs_evidence

rejection_codes.meaning: a non-orchestrator accepted sequence, integration or supersession
rejection_codes.remedy: revert the acceptance and submit a proposal to loop_planning

rejection_codes.meaning: method or tactic exceeds evidenced maturity or prerequisites
rejection_codes.remedy: select a viable alternative or satisfy prerequisites

rejection_codes.meaning: tactic lacks required external catalog metadata
rejection_codes.remedy: use the base method or complete P3 metadata without editing the prompt

rejection_codes.meaning: more than two tactics were selected for one pillar in one cycle
rejection_codes.remedy: retain the highest-ranked one or two and defer the rest

rejection_codes.meaning: canonical prompt count, path or content changed
rejection_codes.remedy: restore the anchored 100-prompt baseline before routing

rejection_codes.meaning: consumer started before a required validated producer handoff
rejection_codes.remedy: complete and validate the dependency

rejection_codes.meaning: parallel tasks share ownership, dependencies or write sets
rejection_codes.remedy: serialize or isolate the work

rejection_codes.meaning: parallel outputs target the same state field
rejection_codes.remedy: return both proposals to loop_planning for owner-based resolution

rejection_codes.meaning: decision table cannot resolve equally supported conflict
rejection_codes.remedy: collect discriminating evidence or design a Refinar test

rejection_codes.meaning: role decided a field owned by another role
rejection_codes.remedy: revert the field and issue a proposal to its owner

rejection_codes.meaning: handoff lacks a required field
rejection_codes.remedy: complete the handoff contract

rejection_codes.meaning: handoff targets an obsolete revision
rejection_codes.remedy: regenerate or revalidate against current state

rejection_codes.meaning: handoff omits required next-role non-decisions
rejection_codes.remedy: populate scope boundary from the owner matrix

rejection_codes.meaning: handoff objective asks target to decide outside its ownership
rejection_codes.remedy: split or reroute the objective

rejection_codes.meaning: handoff contains unsourced fact or unmarked hypothesis
rejection_codes.remedy: attach provenance or relabel

rejection_codes.meaning: one or more required cross-validation checks failed
rejection_codes.remedy: perform each check's required action and rerun

rejection_codes.meaning: Refinar classification lacks comparable benchmark, complete interval or required evidence
rejection_codes.remedy: apply precedence table or classify TESTAR

rejection_codes.meaning: experiment transition lacks required event evidence
rejection_codes.remedy: retain current state until the event is proven

rejection_codes.meaning: transition skips, reverses or leaves the state machine
rejection_codes.remedy: use the next allowed transition or append invalidation/new experiment

rejection_codes.meaning: state was advanced by narration rather than event evidence
rejection_codes.remedy: restore the last evidenced state and append an audit correction

rejection_codes.meaning: route was treated as permission for external mutation
rejection_codes.remedy: obtain operation-specific authorization or remain read-only

rejection_codes.meaning: non-normative hypothesis altered a route
rejection_codes.remedy: remove it from execution or promote it through a recorded decision

examples.name: parallel message and audience foundations before amplification
insufficient evidence does not invent a bottleneck

next_action.objective: audit available data and define collection requirements

examples.name: advanced tactic is maturity-gated without deleting it

expected.action: use Orientar base method and recommend prerequisites

examples.name: contact collision respects suppression before cadence

expected.reason: Orientar suppression precedes experiment allocation and delivery priority

examples.name: second tactic requires a distinct output

expected.condition: distinct non-overlapping outputs and catalog collision check pass

hypotheses.statement: The conjunctive advanced and mature gates plus a two-signal developing gate will reduce over-routing without under-serving most historical projects.
hypotheses.validation_needed: replay against representative old project states in P7 and measure false blocks and unsafe recommendations

hypotheses.statement: Classifying the 0.50-to-0.60 performance interval as OTIMIZAR with critical_recovery will be more useful than TESTAR while preserving the evidence burden for PARAR.
hypotheses.normative_effect: none beyond the proposed P2 decision table
hypotheses.validation_needed: benchmark against historical decisions and review opportunity-cost outcomes

hypotheses.statement: Failing closed to the specialist base method when tactic metadata is absent will preserve quality better than treating unmarked tactics as universally eligible.
hypotheses.validation_needed: P3 catalog coverage test across all 100 prompts and P7 route-recall evaluation

hypotheses.statement: Returning an ambiguous bottleneck to Refinar minimum-viable data audit will produce fewer arbitrary routes than a fixed pillar tie-break order.
hypotheses.validation_needed: compare deterministic fallback outcomes on ambiguous multi-pillar scenarios

unresolved_questions.question: Will P3 provide complete minimum_maturity, prerequisite, contraindication, dependency and collision metadata for all 100 tactics?
unresolved_questions.safe_default_until_resolved: select no tactic with incomplete metadata and run the pillar base method

unresolved_questions.question: Should cancelled and invalidated become canonical v2 experiment states or compatibility-layer terminal dispositions beside the five legacy labels?
unresolved_questions.owner_phase: P2 compatibility policy and P4 state schema
unresolved_questions.safe_default_until_resolved: preserve the explicit state-machine events in v2 and never encode cancellation as completed

unresolved_questions.question: Which statistical method and metric-specific defaults determine minimum viable sample and material improvement?
unresolved_questions.safe_default_until_resolved: require a predeclared method and classify TESTAR when sufficiency cannot be proven

unresolved_questions.question: Does the project corpus support the proposed deterministic maturity thresholds without excessive false gating?
unresolved_questions.safe_default_until_resolved: apply the proposed conservative gates and record every maturity block for evaluation

unresolved_questions.question: What exact state fields and merge semantics will P4 expose for compare-and-swap integration?
unresolved_questions.safe_default_until_resolved: parallel outputs remain staged and loop_planning performs a single integration against an unchanged revision
